proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.

analyses.result_summary: ORR on midostaurin = 0.169 vs off = 0.169; chi-square p=0.92.
ORR on gilteritinib = 0.163 vs off = 0.170; chi-square p=0.13.
ORR on ivosidenib = 0.171 vs off = 0.169; chi-square p=0.76.
ORR on enasidenib = 0.161 vs off = 0.170; chi-square p=0.17.
ORR on ven/aza = 0.174 vs off = 0.166; chi-square p=0.031 — modest positive marginal effect.
ORR on 7+3 = 0.170 vs off = 0.168; chi-square p=0.54.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with idh1_mutation have a higher objective_response rate than IDH1-wildtype patients.
Patients with npm1_mutation have a higher objective_response rate than NPM1-wildtype patients.
Patients with tp53_mutation have a lower objective_response rate than TP53-wildtype patients.
Patients with complex_karyotype have a lower objective_response rate than non-complex-karyotype patients.
Patients with flt3_itd have a different objective_response rate than FLT3-ITD-negative patients.
Patients with idh2_mutation have a different objective_response rate than IDH2-wildtype patients.
Patients with secondary_aml have a lower objective_response rate than de-novo AML patients.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than male patients.

analyses.result_summary: ORR IDH1+ = 0.221 vs IDH1- = 0.165; +5.6 pp; chi-square p=3.5e-18 — strong positive main effect.
ORR NPM1+ = 0.173 vs NPM1- = 0.167; +0.6 pp; p=0.11 — not significant marginally.
ORR TP53+ = 0.166 vs TP53- = 0.169; difference -0.4 pp; p=0.52.
ORR CK+ = 0.168 vs CK- = 0.169; p=0.82 — no marginal effect.
ORR secondary AML = 0.169 vs de-novo = 0.169; p=0.91.
ORR female = 0.171 vs male = 0.168; p=0.33.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative log-odds slope).
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG PS log-odds slope = -0.384 per unit; p=1.8e-97 — strong negative prognostic.
Blast% slope = -0.0057 per percentage point; p=1.2e-19 — higher blasts associated with worse response.
Albumin slope = +0.115 per g/dL; p=1.4e-6 — higher albumin associated with better response.
Weight-loss slope = -0.040 per pp; p=7.4e-35 — strong negative association.
CRP slope = -0.0070 per mg/L; p=2.8e-6.
WBC slope = -0.0022 per k/uL; p=1.9e-5.
Age slope = +0.0004 per year; p=0.72 — no marginal age effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FLT3-ITD-positive patients gain more benefit from treatment_midostaurin than FLT3-ITD-negative patients (positive interaction on objective_response).
FLT3-TKD-positive patients gain more benefit from treatment_midostaurin than FLT3-TKD-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.105, p=0.22. ORR(FLT3-ITD+, mido+) = 0.161 vs (FLT3-ITD+, mido-) = 0.174 — no positive synergy detected.
Interaction coef = +0.023, p=0.87 — no detectable benefit specific to FLT3-TKD+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FLT3-ITD-positive patients gain more benefit from treatment_gilteritinib than FLT3-ITD-negative patients.
FLT3-TKD-positive patients gain more benefit from treatment_gilteritinib than FLT3-TKD-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.034, p=0.69. ORR(FLT3-ITD+, gilt+) = 0.169 vs (FLT3-ITD+, gilt-) = 0.172 — no synergy.
Interaction coef = -0.152, p=0.33 — no significant benefit specific to FLT3-TKD.

proposed_hypotheses.text: IDH1-mutated patients gain greater benefit from treatment_ivosidenib than IDH1-wildtype patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.364, p=0.027 — opposite direction. ORR(IDH1+, ivo+) = 0.174 vs (IDH1+, ivo-) = 0.225, while ORR(IDH1-, ivo+) = 0.171 vs (IDH1-, ivo-) = 0.164. Ivosidenib does not improve response in IDH1+ in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: IDH2-mutated patients gain greater benefit from treatment_enasidenib than IDH2-wildtype patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.124, p=0.45. ORR(IDH2+, ena+) = 0.144 vs (IDH2+, ena-) = 0.167. No drug-target synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: NPM1-mutated patients gain greater benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine than NPM1-wildtype patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.261, p=6.7e-7. ORR(NPM1+, ven/aza+) = 0.193 vs (NPM1+, ven/aza-) = 0.160; ORR(NPM1-, ven/aza+) = 0.165 vs (NPM1-, ven/aza-) = 0.169. Strong positive synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: IDH1-mutated patients gain greater benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine than IDH1-wildtype patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.730, p=7.7e-18. ORR(IDH1+, ven/aza+) = 0.298 vs (IDH1+, ven/aza-) = 0.171; ORR(IDH1-, ven/aza+) = 0.164 vs (IDH1-, ven/aza-) = 0.166. Very strong positive synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Complex-karyotype patients have lower objective_response when treated with treatment_7plus3 than patients without complex karyotype (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.213, p=1.6e-3. ORR(CK+, 7+3+) = 0.155 vs (CK+, 7+3-) = 0.179; ORR(CK-, 7+3+) = 0.173 vs (CK-, 7+3-) = 0.166. Complex karyotype patients fare worse on intensive 7+3 in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: TP53-mutated patients gain less benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine than TP53-wildtype patients (negative interaction).
TP53-mutated patients gain less benefit from treatment_7plus3 than TP53-wildtype patients (negative interaction).
IDH2-mutated patients gain greater benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine than IDH2-wildtype patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.087, p=0.28 — no significant TP53-modifying effect on ven/aza response.
Interaction coef = -0.044, p=0.59 — no significant TP53 × 7+3 effect.
Interaction coef = -0.120, p=0.15 — slight negative direction, not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients aged ≥75 have lower objective_response on treatment_7plus3 relative to younger patients on treatment_7plus3 (negative age × 7+3 interaction).
ECOG PS ≥2 patients have lower objective_response on treatment_7plus3 than ECOG <2 patients on treatment_7plus3 (negative interaction).
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive gain greater benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine than fit patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.041, p=0.45 — no detectable age × 7+3 modification.
Interaction coef = -0.014, p=0.85 — ECOG penalty applies regardless of 7+3.

Interaction coef = +0.104, p=0.036. ORR(unfit, ven/aza+) = 0.179 vs (unfit, ven/aza-) = 0.163; ORR(fit, ven/aza+) = 0.170 vs (fit, ven/aza-) = 0.168. Modest positive interaction — unfit patients derive more benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ECOG, blasts, albumin, weight loss, CRP, WBC, age, sex, and major mutations, idh1_mutation remains an independently positive predictor of objective_response.
After adjustment as above, ecog_ps remains a strong independent negative predictor.
After adjustment as above, blast_pct_marrow remains an independent negative predictor.
After adjustment as above, albumin_g_dl remains an independent positive predictor.
After adjustment as above, weight_loss_pct_6mo remains an independent negative predictor.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit on prognostic feature set (no treatments)
analyses.result_summary: IDH1 coef=+0.360 (p<1e-16), ECOG coef=-0.383 (p<1e-90), blast% coef=-0.0057 (p<1e-19), albumin coef=+0.118 (p=1.4e-6), weight-loss coef=-0.040 (p<1e-30), CRP coef=-0.0073 (p=1.2e-6), WBC coef=-0.0022 (p<1e-4). Sex, age, secondary AML, complex karyotype, FLT3-ITD/TKD, IDH2, TP53 not independently significant. NPM1 coef=+0.043 (p=0.10) borderline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who are simultaneously idh1_mutation-positive AND npm1_mutation-positive have markedly higher objective_response when treated with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine than would be predicted from the additive combination of either mutation alone (positive 3-way interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Triple-interaction coef = +2.144, p=1.7e-29 (and +2.20 in fully adjusted MV model with prognostics, p=4.2e-30). Among patients positive for both IDH1 and NPM1, ORR jumps from 0.168 (off ven/aza) to 0.621 (on ven/aza), n=427 on-arm vs 620 off-arm. The marginal IDH1 × ven/aza interaction is largely explained by this triple-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: defining a binary IDH1+/NPM1+ co-mutation flag, patients with this flag who receive treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have substantially higher objective_response than IDH1+/NPM1+ patients not on ven/aza (large positive interaction).
Within the IDH1+/NPM1+/ven/aza-treated subgroup, higher ecog_ps is still associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +2.111, p=1.0e-45. ORR(dpos, ven/aza+) = 0.621 (n=427) vs (dpos, ven/aza-) = 0.168 (n=620); ORR(non-dpos, ven/aza+) = 0.164 vs (non-dpos, ven/aza-) = 0.166. Co-mutation status is the key effect-modifier for ven/aza response.

analyses.code: Subgroup ORR by ecog_ps among IDH1+/NPM1+/ven-aza+
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: ORR 0.684 (n=152); ECOG 1: 0.644 (n=205); ECOG 2: 0.414 (n=70). Monotonic decline; ECOG retains its negative prognostic role even within this responder-enriched subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race/ethnicity (race_ethnicity) is associated with objective_response.
After adjusting for prognostic features, Black patients have lower objective_response than white patients.
Insurance type (insurance_type) is associated with objective_response.
Rural residence (rural_residence=1) is associated with different objective_response than urban residence.

analyses.result_summary: ORR white=0.173, hispanic=0.167, asian=0.163, black=0.157, other=0.147; chi-square p=0.0096 — small but significant unadjusted disparity.

analyses.code: Logit with race dummies (white reference) + prognostics
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted Black coef = -0.110 (p=0.0048); other coef = -0.195 (p=0.032); hispanic and asian not significant. Disparity for Black patients persists after adjustment.
Unadjusted chi-square p=0.54; adjusted private vs medicare coef=-0.041 (p=0.12), medicaid coef=-0.022 (p=0.55), uninsured coef=-0.031 (p=0.62). No insurance effect detected.
ORR rural=0.169 vs urban=0.169; p=1.0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the 24 SNPs in the dataset, none individually predicts objective_response after multiple-comparison correction (Bonferroni alpha = 0.05/24 = 0.0021).
snp_rs1050828 carriers have a different objective_response than non-carriers.

analyses.code: chi2 each SNP × ORR; count significant at 0.05 and at Bonferroni
analyses.result_summary: Of 24 SNPs, 1 was significant at uncorrected p<0.05 (snp_rs1050828, p=0.0056); none survived Bonferroni correction (threshold ≈0.0021). Consistent with chance.
ORR carriers=0.185 vs non=0.168; uncorrected p=0.0056. Borderline; flagged for replication only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cardiac comorbidities (heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation) reduce objective_response.
Pulmonary comorbidities (copd, interstitial_lung_disease_history) reduce objective_response.
Viral comorbidities (hepatitis_b_history, hepatitis_c_history, hiv_positive) reduce objective_response.
Chronic kidney disease reduces objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Heart failure: ORR diff -0.002 p=0.72; CAD: -0.003 p=0.46; atrial_fib: -0.014 p=0.025 (only AF crosses 0.05, modest).
COPD: -0.006 p=0.14; ILD: -0.006 p=0.54. No significant pulmonary effect.
HepB: -0.004 p=0.75; HepC: +0.007 p=0.48; HIV+: -0.028 p=0.11. No significant viral effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Solid-tumor genomic alterations (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss, msi_high) — biologically irrelevant to AML — do not predict objective_response.
Metastasis flags borrowed from solid-tumor schemas (liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, pericardial_effusion, contralateral_lung_mets) do not predict objective_response.

analyses.code: chi2 each non-AML genomic feature × ORR
analyses.result_summary: All 13 features non-significant (p-values 0.16–0.99); no individual marker reaches 0.05. Consistent with these being inert distractor columns in this AML cohort.
All 6 metastasis features non-significant (p=0.22–0.97). No predictive value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tumor-marker labs from solid-tumor panels (ca_125_u_ml, cea_ng_ml, psa_ng_ml, tsh_uiu_ml) do not predict objective_response in AML.

Vital signs (systolic_bp_mmhg, diastolic_bp_mmhg, heart_rate_bpm, spo2_pct) and serum electrolytes (sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, glucose_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl) do not predict objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: CA-125 p=0.95, CEA p=0.52, PSA p=0.12, TSH p=0.87. None significant — solid-tumor markers do not transfer.
All p>0.10 (sBP 0.22, dBP 0.96, HR 0.90, SpO2 0.39, Na 0.36, K 0.36, Ca 0.92, glucose 0.83, BUN 0.42). Two borderline lab signals: alk-phos p=0.048 (coef +5e-4), AST p=0.094.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher symptom burden (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code: Univariate Logit ORR ~ each symptom grade and dichotomized >=2
analyses.result_summary: fatigue coef +0.014 p=0.21; pain +0.004 p=0.34; dyspnea -0.020 p=0.07; cough -0.010 p=0.35; appetite_loss -0.023 p=0.038. Dichotomized at >=2 none significant. Modest signal only for appetite loss.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
Greater years_since_diagnosis is associated with lower objective_response.
History of prior chemotherapy/radiation/surgery/immunotherapy/targeted_therapy individually does not predict objective_response in this AML cohort.

analyses.result_summary: coef +0.007 per line, p=0.51 — no detectable effect.
coef -0.014 per year, p=0.056 — borderline negative trend.
prior chemo p=0.60, prior radiation p=0.13, prior surgery p=0.12, prior immunotherapy p=0.51, prior targeted p=0.82. Consistent with hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: in a multivariable logistic regression containing prognostic covariates and treatments, the only treatment effect that remains significant marginally is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive).

Refined: in a multivariable model that also includes treatment-by-biomarker interactions, the largest interaction terms are (a) the IDH1 × NPM1 × ven/aza triple-product (positive), (b) IDH1 × ven/aza (positive, but absorbed once triple is added), (c) NPM1 × ven/aza (positive, partly absorbed), and (d) complex_karyotype × 7+3 (negative).

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit with treatments and prognostics
analyses.result_summary: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine coef=+0.059 p=0.016 (significant). Other treatment main effects non-significant: midostaurin -0.005 p=0.88, gilteritinib -0.052 p=0.13, ivosidenib +0.018 p=0.68, enasidenib -0.056 p=0.22, 7+3 +0.017 p=0.48. Confirms hypothesis: only ven/aza shows an unconditional adjusted main effect.

analyses.code: MV Logit including main effects, prognostics, and interaction terms (ck×7p3, idh1×ivo, npm1×venaza, idh1×venaza, idh1×npm1, triple)
analyses.result_summary: Triple-product coef=+2.20 p=4e-30 (largest); ck × 7p3 coef=-0.213 p=0.002. Once the triple is included, 2-way idh1×venaza shrinks to -0.047 p=0.68 and npm1×venaza to +0.040 p=0.48. idh1×ivo coef=-0.293 p=0.094. Conclusion: the IDH1+/NPM1+ co-mutation × ven/aza three-way effect is the dominant treatment-biomarker interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an IDH1-mutant patient who is NOT on ven/aza, treatment_ivosidenib still does not improve objective_response compared with no ivosidenib (i.e. the IDH1×ivosidenib non-effect is not due to confounding with ven/aza).
Higher blast_pct_marrow modifies the ven/aza effect (i.e. blast% × ven/aza interaction).

analyses.code: ORR within IDH1+ & ven/aza-, by ivosidenib
analyses.result_summary: n=2189 IDH1+ off ven/aza. ORR ivo+=0.130 (n=185) vs ivo-=0.175 (n=2004); chi-square p=0.14. Ivosidenib remains non-beneficial in this subgroup.
Interaction coef = +0.0021 per pp, p=0.092. Direction: the negative blast% effect is slightly attenuated under ven/aza, but does not reach significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesizing across iterations: the AML cohort's objective_response is governed by (i) classical AML prognostics (ECOG, blast%, albumin, weight loss, CRP, WBC), (ii) a strong IDH1 main effect and an IDH1 × NPM1 × ven/aza three-way synergy that produces dramatically elevated response (~62%) in the IDH1+/NPM1+/ven-aza-treated triple-positive subgroup, (iii) a negative complex_karyotype × 7+3 interaction, (iv) a modest unfit_for_intensive × ven/aza positive interaction, (v) a small adjusted disparity in ORR for Black patients vs white, and (vi) no detectable benefit from FLT3 inhibitors in FLT3-ITD+/TKD+ patients, no benefit of ivosidenib for IDH1+, and no benefit of enasidenib for IDH2+.

analyses.result_summary: All sub-claims supported by prior tests. Strongest adjusted effects (sorted by |z|): ECOG (-0.39, p<1e-90), weight_loss (-0.041, p<1e-30), triple IDH1×NPM1×ven/aza (+2.20, p<1e-30), blast% (-0.0058, p<1e-19), IDH1 main (when interactions absent: +0.36, p<1e-16), albumin (+0.12, p<1e-6), CRP (-0.0073, p<1e-6), WBC (-0.0022, p<1e-4), ck × 7+3 (-0.21, p=2e-3). None of the unfit × ven/aza or Black-disparity effects exceed |z|≈2.8.
